repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: null-annihilator / row-space criterion for nonunique proximal bridges

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023: proximal counterfactual means can be identified without unique bridge functions

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: prior proximal completeness phase-transition angle; current rho_n is a local weighted certificate refinement

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 plus AndrewsCheng2012: strong functionals under weak nuisance ID and general weak-ID regimes
SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025: confidence sets for integral-equation linear functionals must widen toward the parameter range under weak dependence

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The generic triangular-array flagship claim only gives a matrix witness T=diag(1,c/sqrt(n)); it does not exhibit a realizable finite-support proximal observed law with named atoms, exclusions, T, lambda, and Omega.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The confidence-set comparison misses Santos2012 on NPIV partial-identification inference and Hong2017-style conditional-moment partial-ID inference, both closer to projection confidence sets than the weak-IV survey alone.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to clear flagship, the rho_n frontier needs an open-class proximal law witness or strict comparator-facing non-subsumption theorem, not only a finite Gaussian-shift matrix calculation.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Omega^dagger is assumed positive definite only on a span, but the definitions use ordinary Omega^{-1} and Omega^{-1/2}; require full-rank covariance on the moment coordinate space or state a Moore-Penrose inverse convention.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the matrix-only weak-proxy witness with a finite-support proximal law: list atoms for (Y,A,Z,W,X,U), probabilities, bridge dictionaries, and verify it produces T=diag(1,c/sqrt(n)) and Omega=I.
Add Santos2012 and Hong2017 as partial-identification / conditional-moment confidence-set comparators, then state exactly why Q_n is not subsumed by their sieve-minimum or KS-style procedures.

Strengthen Conjecture 1 into a comparator-facing open-class statement: specify an open neighborhood of observed laws, a computable rho_n functional, and a Le Cam lower-bound route showing iff shrinkage against Bennett/Smucler/Andrews rather than only analogy.

recommended_next_step: Patch the covariance convention, add a genuine finite-support proximal witness, and sharpen the partial-ID inference literature comparison before resubmitting for flagship review.
